with:
  # Build clauses
      clauses: list[str] = [_clause(t.subject, t.predicate, _clean(t.object)) for t in triples]
  
      # Helper to drop repeated subject at the start of a clause
      def _drop_subject(clause: str, subject: str) -> str:
          if clause.lower().startswith(subject.lower()):
              return clause[len(subject):].lstrip()
          return clause
  
      # Trim repeated subjects
      trimmed: list[str] = [_drop_subject(c, triples[i].subject) for i, c in enumerate(clauses)]
  
      # Lower‑case the first character of every clause except the first
      lowered: list[str] = [trimmed[0]] + [c[0].lower() + c[1:] if c else "" for c in trimmed[1:]]
  
      # Assemble a fluent single sentence
      if len(lowered) == 1:
          sentence = lowered[0]
      elif len(lowered) == 2:
          sentence = f"{lowered[0]}, which {lowered[1]}"
      else:
          # join all but the last with commas, add “and” before the final clause
          middle = ", ".join(lowered[1:-1])
          sentence = f"{lowered[0]}, which {middle}, and {lowered[-1]}"
  
      sentence = sentence.strip()
      if not sentence.endswith("."):
          sentence += "."
      # Capitalise the first character
      return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
